CREATED_FOR: MPNN/ProteinMPNN amino-acid composition QC reference constants

METHODOLOGY_AND_PROVENANCE.purpose: Reference amino-acid composition baselines for ProteinMPNN/MPNN design QC and descriptive enrichment reporting.

sequence_distribution.meaning: Contains pooled global_pct plus per_sequence_mean_pct and per_sequence_sd_pct computed across native sequences.
sequence_distribution.caution: The SD is biological variability across native sequences, not a standard error of the mean and not a bootstrap interval.

global_only.meaning: Contains only pooled residue-level global percentages from an official release-statistics table.
global_only.caution: Cannot estimate natural per-protein variance or length-matched expected spread.

size_only.meaning: Contains only entry and residue totals. No amino-acid distribution is available in this JSON.

calculation_definitions.global_pct: 100 * pooled count of amino acid AA across all selected records / pooled count of the 20 standard amino acids across all selected records, for computed baselines. For TrEMBL, values are official pooled complete-database percentages from the release-statistics page.
calculation_definitions.per_sequence_mean_pct: For each selected sequence, compute 100 * count(AA) / count(20 standard AAs in that sequence); then take the arithmetic mean across selected sequences.
calculation_definitions.per_sequence_sd_pct: Standard deviation across the per-sequence percentages. This describes native protein-to-protein variability; it is not a confidence interval and not a bootstrap-derived SD.
calculation_definitions.total_residues: All residue characters counted in the source records, including nonstandard/ambiguous residues when present.
calculation_definitions.total_standard_residues: Only residues in AA_ORDER. This is the denominator for computed global_pct values.
calculation_definitions.nonstandard_counts: Counts of nonstandard/ambiguous residues observed in computed source sequences; excluded from 20-AA denominators.
calculation_definitions.rounding: Computed percentages are rounded to three decimals. Official TrEMBL global percentages are retained at the precision exposed by the release-statistics table, mostly two decimals.

enzyme_selection_method.ec_annotated_baseline: Swiss-Prot flatfile records were selected if at least one DE-line contained an EC= annotation.
enzyme_selection_method.ec_class_baselines: Top-level EC classes were inferred from EC numbers with first component 1-7.
enzyme_selection_method.multi_ec_handling: Records with EC annotations from multiple top-level classes are counted in each matching class baseline. EC-class baselines are therefore not mutually exclusive and should not be summed.

recommended_mpnn_workflow: Run global and per-sequence amino-acid composition checks against Swiss-Prot EC for enzyme designs.
If top-level EC class is known, also compare against the matching EC-class baseline.
Use TrEMBL global-only as a broad unreviewed-protein-space descriptive comparator, not as a statistical gate.
Exclude fixed catalytic/motif residues from composition QC when they are intentionally constrained.
Prefer burial/core/surface-stratified checks when structural exposure labels are available.

audit_limitations: This JSON preserves numeric values from the supplied baseline file and adds provenance/method metadata; it does not recompute new values.
Swiss-Prot official release statistics provide rounded pooled global composition; the per-sequence mean/SD fields are computed-derived values, not official UniProt-published tables.
No trustworthy current public per-sequence mean/SD amino-acid tables were found for UniRef50, UniRef90, UniRef100, nr-aa, or RefSeq peptide, so those entries remain size-only.
For strict regulatory or publication use, regenerate these constants from release-pinned FASTA/flatfile downloads and store file checksums.

uniprotkb_documentation.name: UniProtKB documentation
uniprotkb_documentation.description: Documents UniProtKB as two sections: reviewed/manual UniProtKB/Swiss-Prot and unreviewed/computational UniProtKB/TrEMBL.
used_for: Definitions of Swiss-Prot reviewed and TrEMBL unreviewed records.

swissprot_relstat_2026_01.name: UniProtKB/Swiss-Prot release 2026_01 statistics
swissprot_relstat_2026_01.description: Official Swiss-Prot release statistics page. Provides release totals and rounded pooled amino-acid composition for the complete reviewed database.
used_for: Independent source for Swiss-Prot n_sequences and total_residues.
Independent check of Swiss-Prot global amino-acid percentages after rounding.

swissprot_fasta_current_release.name: UniProtKB/Swiss-Prot current_release FASTA
swissprot_fasta_current_release.description: Release-pinned Swiss-Prot FASTA sequence records from the UniProt FTP current_release area at the time these constants were generated.
used_for: Computed all-reviewed Swiss-Prot per-residue pooled composition.
Computed per-sequence mean and standard deviation of 20-AA composition.
Counted nonstandard residues B, O, U, X, and Z.

swissprot_flatfile_current_release.name: UniProtKB/Swiss-Prot current_release flatfile
swissprot_flatfile_current_release.description: Release-pinned Swiss-Prot flatfile records from the UniProt FTP current_release area at the time these constants were generated.
used_for: Selected enzyme records using DE-line EC= annotations.
Assigned enzyme records to top-level EC classes 1-7.
Computed enzyme and EC-class amino-acid composition distributions.

trembl_relstat_2026_01.name: UniProtKB/TrEMBL current release statistics
trembl_relstat_2026_01.description: Official TrEMBL current release statistics page/embedded relstat HTML. Provides pooled residue percentages only; no public per-sequence mean or SD table was found.

genomenet_dbget_release_table.name: GenomeNet DBGET database release information
genomenet_dbget_release_table.description: Current DBGET table listing database release IDs, entry counts, and residue counts for nr-aa, RefSeq peptide, UniProtKB, TrEMBL, UniRef50, UniRef90, UniRef100, and related databases.
used_for: Independent source for large-dataset entry and residue totals.
Size-only metadata where no trustworthy public amino-acid distribution table was found.

uniref_documentation.name: UniRef documentation
uniref_documentation.description: Documents UniRef100, UniRef90, and UniRef50 as clustered sequence resources derived from UniProtKB/UniParc sequence space.
used_for: Definition of UniRef clustered datasets; not a source of amino-acid composition percentages.

uniprotkb_swissprot_reviewed_2026_01.source_note: Computed from UniProtKB/Swiss-Prot current_release FASTA; 20 standard amino acids only in denominator.
uniprotkb_swissprot_reviewed_2026_01.description: All reviewed UniProtKB/Swiss-Prot protein sequences in release 2026_01; curated native protein-space baseline.

method.selection_rule: All reviewed Swiss-Prot records in the release.
method.denominator_rule: Only the 20 standard amino acids in AA_ORDER are used for global_pct and per-sequence percentage denominators.
method.nonstandard_handling: B, O, U, X, and Z were counted separately in nonstandard_counts and excluded from 20-AA percentages.
method.per_sequence_mean_pct_formula: Mean over sequences of 100 * sequence_AA_count / sequence_standard_AA_count.
method.per_sequence_sd_pct_formula: Standard deviation over sequences of 100 * sequence_AA_count / sequence_standard_AA_count.
method.official_cross_check: n_sequences, total_residues, and rounded global_pct are consistent with the official Swiss-Prot 2026_01 release-statistics page.

recommended_use: General curated native protein-space comparator.
Statistical QC baseline when enzyme-specific or EC-class-specific baseline is not appropriate.
limitations: Reviewed Swiss-Prot is curated and nonredundant-ish but much smaller than TrEMBL/UniRef; not representative of all metagenomic/unreviewed sequence space.

quality_control.expected_global_pct_sum_after_rounding: Approximately 100.0; minor deviations reflect rounded percentages.

uniprotkb_swissprot_reviewed_ec_annotate.source_note: Computed from UniProtKB/Swiss-Prot flatfile records with at least one DE-line EC= annotation; 20 standard amino acids only in denominator.
uniprotkb_swissprot_reviewed_ec_annotate.description: Reviewed Swiss-Prot proteins with at least one EC annotation; curated enzyme-space baseline.

method.selection_rule: Record included if at least one DE-line contained an EC= annotation.
method.denominator_rule: Only the 20 standard amino acids in AA_ORDER are used for global_pct and per-sequence percentage denominators.
method.nonstandard_handling: B, O, U, X, and Z were counted separately in nonstandard_counts and excluded from 20-AA percentages.
method.per_sequence_mean_pct_formula: Mean over EC-annotated sequences of 100 * sequence_AA_count / sequence_standard_AA_count.
method.per_sequence_sd_pct_formula: Standard deviation over EC-annotated sequences of 100 * sequence_AA_count / sequence_standard_AA_count.

recommended_use: Default baseline for enzyme-design MPNN/ProteinMPNN QC.
Use for z-scores or warning thresholds when no EC-class-specific baseline is more appropriate.
limitations: Only enzymes that are reviewed and EC-annotated in Swiss-Prot are included; enzyme classes and taxa are not equally represented.

quality_control.expected_global_pct_sum_after_rounding: Approximately 100.0; minor deviations reflect rounded percentages.

uniprotkb_swissprot_reviewed_ec_class_1_.source_note: Computed from Swiss-Prot records containing at least one EC=1.* annotation. Multi-EC proteins may appear in multiple class baselines.
uniprotkb_swissprot_reviewed_ec_class_1_.description: Reviewed Swiss-Prot EC-annotated enzyme subset for top-level EC class 1 (oxidoreductases); curated class-specific enzyme baseline.

method.selection_rule: Record included if at least one parsed EC number had top-level class 1 (that is, EC=1.x.x.x). Proteins with EC annotations in multiple top-level classes may appear in multiple EC-class baselines.
method.denominator_rule: Only the 20 standard amino acids in AA_ORDER are used for global_pct and per-sequence percentage denominators.
method.nonstandard_handling: Nonstandard/ambiguous residues listed in nonstandard_counts were excluded from 20-AA percentages.
method.global_pct_formula: 100 * pooled_AA_count / total_standard_residues across records in this EC class.
method.per_sequence_mean_pct_formula: Mean over selected EC-class sequences of 100 * sequence_AA_count / sequence_standard_AA_count.
method.per_sequence_sd_pct_formula: Standard deviation over selected EC-class sequences of 100 * sequence_AA_count / sequence_standard_AA_count.
method.mutual_exclusivity: False; EC-class baselines can overlap because multi-EC proteins are included in every matching top-level EC class.

recommended_use: Class-specific comparator for designs whose target enzyme belongs to EC class 1 (oxidoreductases).
Use alongside the all-EC enzyme baseline rather than as a replacement when sample size is a concern.
limitations: Not mutually exclusive across EC classes.
May be biased by Swiss-Prot review/annotation coverage and by overrepresented enzyme families.

quality_control.expected_global_pct_sum_after_rounding: Approximately 100.0; minor deviations reflect rounded percentages.

uniprotkb_trembl_unreviewed_2026_01_glob.source_note: Official UniProtKB/TrEMBL Current Release Statistics embedded relstat.html. Pooled residue frequencies only; no per-sequence mean/SD available from the public stats page.
uniprotkb_trembl_unreviewed_2026_01_glob.description: All unreviewed UniProtKB/TrEMBL protein sequences in release 2026_01; huge unreviewed protein-space global composition comparator only.

method.input_records: No local per-sequence computation was performed for this JSON entry.
method.selection_rule: All UniProtKB/TrEMBL records represented in the official 2026_01 release-statistics table.
method.denominator_rule: Official complete-database pooled percentages as reported by the TrEMBL release-statistics page.
method.nonstandard_handling: Official release-statistics page reports B, Z, and X percentages; no per-residue count table or per-sequence distribution is included here.
method.global_pct_formula: Official pooled residue percentage from UniProtKB/TrEMBL release statistics, not recomputed here.
method.qc_rule: Descriptive enrichment comparison only; do not use for z-scores, empirical p-values, or pass/fail QC.

recommended_use: Broad descriptive comparison against huge unreviewed protein space.
Useful to show whether a design batch is globally enriched/depleted relative to TrEMBL.
limitations: Global-only; no sequence-level variance.
Unreviewed TrEMBL is more redundant and more computationally annotated than Swiss-Prot.
Not length-matched, enzyme-matched, taxonomy-matched, or structure-context-matched.

quality_control.expected_global_pct_sum_after_rounding: Approximately 100.0; minor deviations reflect rounded percentages.

nr_aa_2026_04_04.description: Large protein sequence database size metadata only; no amino-acid composition distribution included in this JSON.

method.qc_rule: Do not use for composition comparison or statistical QC until amino-acid distributions are recomputed from sequence files.

recommended_use: Provenance/scale context only.
Use to decide whether a future local streaming recomputation is worth doing.
limitations: No amino-acid percentages are included.
Cannot compute enrichment, z-scores, empirical p-values, or pass/fail QC from this entry alone.

baseline_selection_priority: If enzyme design and EC class is known: use matching uniprotkb_swissprot_reviewed_ec_class_*_2026_01 plus uniprotkb_swissprot_reviewed_ec_annotated_2026_01.
If enzyme design but EC class is unknown: use uniprotkb_swissprot_reviewed_ec_annotated_2026_01.
If general protein design: use uniprotkb_swissprot_reviewed_2026_01.
Use uniprotkb_trembl_unreviewed_2026_01_global_only only as a descriptive huge-database comparator.
hard_rules: Only compute z-scores or pass/fail thresholds when baseline_type == sequence_distribution and statistical_qc_allowed == true.
For baseline_type == global_only, compute only descriptive metrics such as delta_pp, enrichment_ratio, and log2_enrichment.
For DATASET_SIZE_ONLY entries, do not compute amino-acid composition metrics.
Always exclude nonstandard residues from the 20-AA denominator and report them separately.
Do not silently compare designed catalytic/fixed motifs against native global composition; support masks for excluded positions.

suggested_default_thresholds.note: These are pragmatic starting thresholds, not derived from the JSON itself. Prefer bootstrapped length-matched nulls if implementing a full QC pipeline.
